story: Iran/Hormuz war and crude supply-shock risk.
reason: Obvious oil-price play; a supply shock benefits tankers (ton-mile expansion, war-risk surcharges) more directly than an integrated major, and any oil-price spike is partly capped/priced-in by spare capacity plus SPR releases. Chose FRO instead as the more direct second-order beneficiary.

story: Global nuclear buildout / reactor construction wave.
reason: Crowded, richly-valued uranium-miner trade; the binding bottleneck in the nuclear buildout is enrichment capacity (Russia/Tenex-dominated), not mined uranium pounds. Chose LEU instead as the less-crowded enrichment play.

story: NATO Ankara summit rearmament commitments.
reason: Named prime contractor; backlog impact already reflected in consensus. Sub-tier suppliers of drones/munitions-enablers/propulsion offer higher second-order beta on broad Western rearmament. Chose KTOS instead.

story: HBM/3D-stacking memory boom (SK Hynix debut, NVIDIA-SK Hynix partnership).
reason: Direct HBM beneficiary but carries memory-price cyclicality and single-product concentration risk. Advanced-packaging metrology (Camtek) captures HBM unit growth without that price exposure. Chose CAMT instead.

story: Record US power demand / AI data-center buildout.
reason: Consensus turbine/AI-power long, already richly valued. Medium-voltage switchgear (Powell Industries) is a less-crowded bottleneck riding the same driver. Chose POWL instead.

story: USDA crop report / fertilizer demand tightness.
reason: Broad fertilizer/potash beta lacks the specific US Henry Hub feedstock arbitrage that widens CF Industries' nitrogen margins against a global gas-price spike. Chose CF instead.

story: LNG-Europe equipment demand -- same trigger considered for EE (Excelerate Energy).
reason: Being acquired by Baker Hughes ($13.6B deal, EU approval already secured); the stock is pinned to deal-arb spread, not free to trade the underlying thesis.

story: Western US drought driving desalination capex.
reason: Company just withdrew its FY26 desalination guidance explicitly because the Iran conflict is hurting its Middle East business -- the news is net negative for the stock, not a clean beneficiary of the story.

story: Leads new Lloyd's Hormuz war-risk insurance consortium and a $40B DFC facility.
reason: Real news, but marine war-risk premium income is a rounding error against a ~$110B diversified insurer -- immaterial to the stock.

story: Data-center behind-the-meter power demand (VA consumption tax, White House AI power-cost event).
reason: Already booked $7.65B in data-center deals in the past 90 days plus a $25B Brookfield deal (5x upsized Jun 30). This is now the consensus AI-power name, not an overlooked second-order play.

story: Gulf drone-attack proliferation (Kuwait, Jordan, Saudi Arabia) driving counter-drone demand.
reason: The $500M Army contract was announced Jul 1-2, nearly two weeks before this sweep window -- already digested by the market.

story: Qatar/China helium supply crisis threatening chip-fab (HBM/DRAM) production.
reason: JPMorgan and Wells Fargo already issued upgrades citing this exposure; helium is a small slice of a ~$70B diversified industrial-gas major.

story: Western US drought driving irrigation-equipment capex.
reason: Management's own Q3 FY26 earnings call said the opposite -- irrigation demand is soft despite severe drought, because weak farm economics and high input costs dominate. Plausible story, contradicted by the company's own disclosure.

story: Domestic helium supply diversification.
reason: Rebranded to New Era Energy & Digital (ticker NUAI) in Aug 2025, pivoting to AI/data-center infrastructure. No longer a clean helium proxy.

story: Copper-scrap economics improving as the refined-copper deficit widens.
reason: Acquired by Toyota Tsusho America and delisted from Nasdaq in July 2025. Not tradable.

story: US grid buildout/data-center demand has pushed grain-oriented electrical steel (GOES) demand and transformer lead times to record levels; Cleveland-Cliffs is the only North American GOES producer, just won a $400M/5-year electrical-steel military contract, and is bringing a new Weirton, WV transformer plant online (+30-40% GOES tonnage).
reason: Real niche, but 'stainless and electrical' combined was just 3% of CLF's Q1 2026 product sales volume -- immaterial to a ~$20B diversified steel producer's overall earnings. The angle is also already circulating in analyst/finance commentary. Story-to-fundamentals ratio too high; no cleaner pure-play GOES/transformer-component beneficiary was found.

story: Farmer/trucker 'mega-blockades' across Mexico (Ciudad Juarez-El Paso crossing stranding ~38,000 truckloads, ~$1.45B in unrealized exports) have periodically snarled cross-border truck freight, theoretically diverting volume to rail.
reason: Sources conflict on whether the blockades are still active as of mid-July (some report the nationwide strike ended in April after clashes with authorities) -- the catalyst's currency is unclear. Even if live, UNP/CPKC are diversified mega-cap railroads already running structural truck-to-rail conversion programs unrelated to this specific unrest; a short-lived disruption is immaterial to earnings, and 'railroads win when trucking is disrupted' is a reflexive association, not a sized causal chain.

story: Renewed Iran strikes and elevated Strait of Hormuz war-risk premiums (~5% of hull value) continue to support tanker economics.
reason: Same Hormuz/tanker-rate driver already captured via the open FRO pick (2026-07-13); STNG/INSW are already being named in mainstream finance media (Benzinga: '3 shipping and insurance stocks to watch') as the obvious plays. Adding a second overlapping tanker name would concentrate the portfolio in an already-represented, already-publicized theme rather than diversify it.

story: Microsoft's July 2026 Patch Tuesday shipped a record 622 CVEs, including an actively-exploited SharePoint zero-day (CVE-2026-56164) that CISA gave federal agencies until July 17 to remediate.
reason: 'Buy vulnerability-management vendors after a big Patch Tuesday' is a reflexive, recurring association (happens most months to some degree), not a distinct causal chain -- exactly the first-association trap the process is meant to screen out. Enterprise security-spend cycles are too diffuse and slow-moving to time off one disclosure event.

story: IEA warned July 16 that full implementation of China's rare-earth export controls could put $6.5T of downstream Western production at risk; China added 10 more US entities, including rare-earth miners, to its own export-control list in June.
reason: MP is the single most obvious, already-consensus rare-earth reshoring trade -- heavily covered for 2+ years and direct/first-order exposure (an actual rare-earth miner/processor) rather than second-order. The existing open pick METC (Ramaco Resources) already provides less-obvious exposure to the same G7 diversification tailwind via its Wyoming rare-earth project; today's IEA headline is logged as a reiteration for METC, not a new pick.

story: Nvidia unveiled Cosmos 3 Edge (robotics/physical-AI world model); Fujitsu, Hitachi and Kawasaki Heavy Industries announced a coalition to build open multimodal foundation models for robotics and physical-AI applications -- machine vision is the sensory layer for this build-out.
reason: CGNX already trades at 77.7x P/E vs. an estimated fair multiple near 42.8x and a 31.2x industry average; Q1 2026 beat (revenue +24% YoY) already drove analyst upgrades and multiple 'is CGNX fully valued' pieces in the financial press. The specific Nvidia/Fujitsu/Hitachi/Kawasaki robotics-coalition news is incremental to an AI-vision optimism narrative that is already fully reflected in the stock, not a fresh, undiscovered catalyst.

story: Guinea implemented bauxite export curbs (~25% cut from 2026 baseline), tightening global alumina supply and raising costs for non-integrated smelters.
reason: Financial press already explicitly frames CENX's stock surge as a 'Hormuz hedge' and covers its accelerated Mt. Holly, SC restart to fill the Middle-East-import vacuum -- this is a well-documented, already-consensus trade, not a second-order read investors haven't made yet. CENX's Jamalco JV also gives it Jamaica-sourced alumina largely insulated from the Guinea curb specifically, muddying the direct benefit.

story: Nickel prices hit a three-week high as Indonesia holds mining quotas steady, tightening battery-metal supply -- considered as a beneficiary via battery-metal recycling (higher scrap-nickel recovery value).
reason: Sub-one-year cash runway, sustained -$25-26M TTM operating/free cash flow, and heavy dilution history (ATM raises plus a new $46.25M shelf; shares outstanding up to 132.3M as of Q1 2026). Fails the going-concern/dilution screen regardless of thesis quality. No other financially healthy, US-major-exchange-listed pure-play nickel producer or recycler was found to substitute (Indonesian and diversified-major nickel exposure is immaterial/first-order for large-cap miners).

story: J&J's $55B pharma-manufacturing reshoring investment and the broader $480B/13-company industry reshoring pledge tied to Section 232 pharmaceutical tariffs (100% default rate, deadlines July 31/Sept 29).
reason: UBS has already explicitly tagged Repligen as 'the bioprocessing leader for 2026' citing this exact reshoring tailwind, raised its price target to $200, and Q1 2026 results (order growth +20%, revenue +15% YoY) already reflect the trend. This is a consensus sell-side thesis, not an overlooked second-order read.

story: Same pharma-reshoring/sterile-injectable tariff wave (West supplies vial-stopper/syringe components for injectable fill-finish).
reason: Stock already up 32.9% YTD vs. S&P's 28.2%, with 21 analysts having lifted price targets and multiple 'reasons to buy' Zacks/TradingView pieces already circulating. Fully reflected in the share price already.

story: Space Force raised the NSSL Phase 3 Lane 1 contract ceiling from $5.6B to $17B (July 17 notice), expanding the pre-approved launch-provider pool to seven companies including Rocket Lab.
reason: Rocket Lab cannot win any individual task order under this vehicle until Neutron completes a successful first flight (targeted Q4 2026) -- there is no near-term revenue mechanism. The stock actually fell >12% on July 16 (down 55% from its 2026 peak) amid broader negative space-sector sentiment, muddying any clean causal read that this specific news is bullish right now.

story: Diesel >$5/gal national average, gasoline inventories at a 14-year seasonal low, and refiners maximizing distillate output -- 3-2-1 crack spread near $69 vs ~$20 at the start of 2026.
reason: Stock already up ~125% YTD; Motley Fool has explicitly written it up as 'a great way to hedge geopolitical conflict'; Zacks 60-day EPS estimate revisions already up 33%. Textbook already-consensus, already-publicized trade.

story: Same refining crack-spread widening story as PBF.
reason: Zacks 60-day EPS estimate revisions already up 40%; Q1 adjusted EBITDA already jumped from $33.6M to $211.7M year over year on the same crack-spread dynamic, and sell-side coverage already frames DK/PBF as the pure-play crack-spread pair trade.

story: Same refining crack-spread story (third independent refiner considered).
reason: Stock up more than 80% YTD; Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs and UBS have all raised price targets within days of each other and Evercore just initiated coverage; a 24/7 Wall St piece is literally titled explaining why refiners 'are on fire' off this exact crack-spread number. Maximally crowded already.

story: Truckload spot rates hit an all-time record ($3.83/mile) and tender rejections climbed to 17.55%, theoretically driving carriers/shippers to Ryder's flexible rental/leasing fleet instead of committing capex to owned equipment.
reason: Ryder's own incoming CEO said the company has 'yet to see meaningful near-term change' in the freight market, calling this the longest rate recession in industry memory -- the company's own disclosure contradicts the plausible-sounding capacity-crunch beneficiary story, the same pattern that killed LNN on drought/irrigation in a prior run.

story: Record truckload spot rates and tight capacity as a potential trigger for a trailer-order upcycle.
reason: The only supporting data (backlog +19% QoQ to $837M) is from the Q1 2026 earnings release in April/May, not fresh news from the last ~24-48h; management itself guides to a market rebound only in 'late 2026 and 2027' and reported a Q1 shipment decline of 14.5% YoY. No genuinely new catalyst this window.

story: Qatar's multi-year LNG export-capacity loss and Europe's LNG scramble (same driver as the open EE pick) considered for a Gulf Coast LNG export developer.
reason: Stock already moved on July 13 explicitly framed as 'avoided the Hormuz shipping crisis'; Goldman Sachs initiated coverage July 15 at Neutral (not bullish); first LNG production isn't expected until H1 2027, well outside a typical hold period, and the company is pre-revenue on the relevant asset -- weak fit versus the viability/timeliness bar.
